title: Detox (Medically-Supported Withdrawal)
description: Medically-supervised detox from alcohol, benzodiazepines, heroin, and fentanyl at Oregon's largest nonprofit addiction treatment provider (since 1973). Same-day admissions available 7 days a week; in-network with most major insurance.
engage: Call admissions at 800-543-9905 — same-day admission is often available, 7 days a week.
content:
Program

Safe, medically-supervised withdrawal management with 24-hour clinical oversight, specializing in alcohol, benzodiazepine, heroin, and fentanyl detox.

Insurance

In-network with most major insurers (Blue Cross, Aetna, Cigna, Kaiser, United Healthcare, TRICARE, and more). CARF-accredited.

title: Residential / Inpatient Treatment
description: On-campus residential addiction treatment for adults 18+ at the Coburg facility — structured therapeutic programming with housing and amenities, in short-term and long-term options.
engage: Call 800-543-9905 for admission (same-day often available). Inpatient campus at 1 Serenity Lane, Coburg.
content:
Program

Intensive, structured residential treatment with on-campus housing, individual and group therapy, and recreational amenities. Short-term and long-term stays available.

Who It's For

Adults 18+ needing structured, live-in care.

title: Medication-Assisted Treatment (MAT)
description: Medication-assisted treatment combining pharmacological support with counseling for opioid and alcohol dependence, available at Serenity Lane's Portland and other Oregon locations.
engage: Call 800-543-9905 to start MAT and verify insurance.
content:
Program

Evidence-based medications paired with counseling to reduce cravings and support recovery from opioid or alcohol use disorder.

title: Outpatient Treatment
description: Outpatient addiction treatment at several intensity levels — standard outpatient, intensive outpatient (IOP), and day treatment — so you can recover while living at home and working. Portland West location plus Albany, Bend, Eugene, Roseburg, and Salem.
engage: Call 800-543-9905 to arrange outpatient treatment at the Portland West clinic (10920 SW Barbur Blvd) or another location.
content:
Levels of Care

  • Level I Outpatient
  • Level II Intensive Outpatient (IOP)
  • Day Treatment

Locations

Portland West, Albany, Bend, Eugene, Roseburg, and Salem.

title: DUII Services
description: Specialized DUII assessment, education, and treatment meeting Oregon court and diversion requirements, for court-ordered or self-referred individuals.
engage: Call 800-543-9905 to enroll in DUII services.
content:
Program

DUII-specific evaluation, alcohol/drug education, and treatment structured to satisfy Oregon court and DMV requirements.

Who It's For

People with DUII charges or diversion requirements.

title: No-Barrier Food Pantry
description: A volunteer-powered, no-barrier food pantry in the St. Johns neighborhood where anyone in need can shop for groceries — no proof of eligibility required. Open Monday and Friday 10am–4pm, Wednesday 10am–12pm.
engage: Walk in during open hours at 8100 N Lombard St, Portland (Mon & Fri 10am–4pm, Wed 10am–12pm). Questions: 503-286-0750 or stjohnsfoodshare@gmail.com.
content:
What It Offers

An open-door, choice-model food pantry — shop for groceries that fit your needs with no proof of eligibility and no judgment. Protein availability varies (meat Mondays, eggs Wednesdays/Fridays when available).

Hours

  • Monday: 10am–4pm
  • Wednesday: 10am–12pm
  • Friday: 10am–4pm

title: Mental Health Therapy
description: Individual, couples, family, and group counseling available statewide by telehealth or in person, including specialized programs for veterans, seniors, domestic violence survivors, and school-aged youth. Offices in Portland, Milwaukie, Beaverton, and across Oregon.
engage: Call 541-900-4285 or email info@strongeroregon.com, or use the provider search at strongeroregon.com. Telehealth and in-person options.
content:
Services

  • Individual, couples, family, and group counseling
  • Veterans services
  • School-based mental health
  • Domestic violence shelter services
  • Senior care and outreach

Access

Statewide via telehealth (phone, desktop, mobile) or in person at offices including Portland, Milwaukie, and Beaverton.

title: Housing & Basic Needs Assistance
description: Help with housing, food, and climate-related benefits alongside mental health support — connecting adults, youth, and families statewide to stability and resources.
engage: Call 541-900-4285 or email info@strongeroregon.com to ask about housing and basic-needs assistance.
content:
Services

Assistance navigating housing, food, and climate/energy benefits, offered together with Stronger Oregon's mental health services to support whole-person stability.

Who It's For

Adults, youth, and families across Oregon.

title: Reentry Mentoring & Family Support
description: Support for justice system-impacted individuals and families — reentry mentoring for justice-involved parents, peer mentoring, case management, and the Center for Family Success in Multnomah County.
engage: Call 503-892-5396 or email info@thepathfindernetwork.org to connect with reentry and family services.
content:
Community Programs

  • Reentry mentoring for justice-involved parents
  • Peer mentoring and case management
  • Early childhood home visiting
  • Intervention programs for children of justice-involved parents
  • Center for Family Success (Multnomah County)

Who It's For

People leaving incarceration, parents on parole/probation, and families impacted by the justice system.

title: Parenting Programs (Parenting Inside Out)
description: Evidence-based parenting skills training — including Parenting Inside Out® for incarcerated mothers and fathers and community parenting classes — helping justice-involved parents strengthen family bonds.
engage: Call 503-892-5396 or email info@thepathfindernetwork.org to learn about parenting classes.
content:
Programs

  • Parenting Inside Out®: evidence-based parenting skills training for incarcerated parents (via Oregon Dept. of Corrections)
  • Community parenting classes for justice-involved parents

Who It's For

Incarcerated and community-based parents impacted by the justice system.

title: Peer-Delivered Recovery & Reentry Support
description: A peer-run nonprofit (formerly Mental Health & Addiction Association of Oregon) supporting recovery and wellness through lived experience — peer support, recovery services, reentry support, advocacy, outreach, and connections to housing, detox, and treatment.
engage: Self-refer through the online form at mhaoforegon.jotform.com, call 503-922-2377, or visit mhaoforegon.org.
content:
Services

  • Peer support and recovery mentoring from people with lived experience
  • Reentry support
  • Advocacy, outreach, and education
  • Connections to housing, detox, and treatment

Who It's For

People on their recovery and wellness journeys, especially those with lived experience of mental health and addiction challenges.

Note

Formerly the Mental Health & Addiction Association of Oregon (MHAAO); renamed The Peer Company in September 2025.

title: Fitness & Movement for Recovery
description: A free, trauma-informed functional-fitness, strength, and endurance gym built specifically for people in or seeking recovery from substance use — high-quality coaching in a supportive, judgment-free community. A project of the Alano Club of Portland. Locations in Portland and Hillsboro.
engage: Join for free at 931 SE 6th Ave, Portland (or Hillsboro). See the class schedule at therecoverygym.org. 100% cost-free for anyone in or seeking recovery.
content:
What It Offers

  • Functional fitness, strength training, and endurance classes
  • Trauma-informed, inclusive coaching
  • A supportive recovery community that celebrates recovery as a visible, healthy lifestyle

Locations

  • Portland: 931 SE 6th Ave
  • Hillsboro: 1060 SW Baseline St

Cost

100% free for anyone in or seeking recovery.

title: LGBTQIA2S+ Recovery Mentoring & Drop-In
description: Free peer recovery mentoring and a welcoming drop-in center centering the LGBTQIA2S+ community. Certified mentors (each with 1+ year in recovery) help with goal-setting, resource navigation, and community connection — embracing all pathways to recovery, including medication-assisted treatment. Drop-in noon–8pm, Sunday–Friday.
engage: Drop in at 3807 NE MLK Blvd, Portland (12–8pm, Sun–Fri), call 971-252-1943, or request a mentor via the form at truecolorsrecovery.org.
content:
Services

  • Peer recovery mentoring from certified LGBTQIA2S+ mentors
  • Goal-setting and life planning
  • Community resource navigation
  • Drop-in center and social connection

Who It's For

LGBTQIA2S+ individuals seeking recovery support. All pathways welcome, including MAT.

Hours

Noon–8pm, Sunday–Friday (closed Saturdays). Also an RRC program in Josephine County.
street_address: 3807 NE Martin Luther King Jr Blvd

title: Food Pantry & Hygiene Items
description: A food pantry providing healthy food and hygiene items to Portland-area neighbors facing food insecurity, at William Temple House's NW Portland office.
engage: Call 503-226-3021 or visit 2023 NW Hoyt St, Portland during food pantry hours.
content:
Services

Healthy groceries and personal hygiene items for community members experiencing food insecurity.

How to Access

Visit during food pantry hours or call 503-226-3021.

title: Mental Health Counseling
description: Professional, affordable mental health counseling for individuals and families, supporting emotional wellness.
engage: Call 503-226-3021 to learn about counseling services.
content:
Services

Professional counseling for individuals and families to support mental and emotional wellness, offered on a sliding scale.

title: Social Services & Utility Assistance
description: Help with utility assistance and other essential support, connecting individuals and families to resources they need.
engage: Call 503-226-3021 to ask about utility assistance and social services.
content:
Services

Utility/energy assistance and additional support resources for individuals and families in need.

title: Thrift Store (Affordable Goods)
description: A thrift store offering affordable clothing, furniture, and household items, with proceeds funding William Temple House's food pantry, counseling, and social services. Open Mon–Sat 10am–6pm, Sun 11am–6pm.
engage: Shop at 2230 NW Glisan St, Portland (Mon–Sat 10am–6pm, Sun 11am–6pm). Phone: 503-222-3328.
content:
What It Offers

Affordable clothing, furniture, and household goods. Purchases support William Temple House's programs.

Hours

Mon–Sat 10am–6pm; Sun 11am–6pm (closed the 3rd Monday).
